rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative sulfoximido-oxygenation of alkenes (styrenes) using NH-sulfoximines and alcohols (MeOH) to give β-alkoxy sulfoximides without metal catalysts or external oxidants
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: NH-sulfoximine (1a) is deprotonated to 1a' then anodically oxidized to an N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical (A); MeOH is also anodically oxidized in the presence of base to a methoxy radical. Cathode reduces 2 H+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzyl/carbon radical adduct after radical addition to styrene (B)
benzyl cation intermediate (C) from further anodic oxidation of B

electron_flow: At the anode, deprotonated NH-sulfoximine (1a') is oxidized to an N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical A, which adds to the styrene to give benzyl radical B; B is further oxidized at the anode to benzyl cation C, which is trapped by MeOH and deprotonated to give product 3a. At the cathode, protons are reduced to H2.

probe_reagents.name: radical clock (cyclopropyl-substituted alkene 7)
probe_reagents.observation: Standard product 8 (21%) and ring-opened product 9 (64%) isolated, confirming a benzyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: control experiment detecting sulfoximidoyl dimer 10 and radical addition adducts 11/12
probe_reagents.observation: Dimer 10 from N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical and adducts 11/12 of methoxy radical detected by HRMS, supporting both N-radical and MeO radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of styrene 2a, MeOH/K3PO4, and 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Ep(2a)=2.07 V vs SCE; Ep(MeOH)=1.29 V; oxidation peaks of MeOH and 1a overlap and are lower than styrene, indicating anodic oxidation of 1a/MeOH occurs preferentially (reaction onset ~0.8 V)

probe_reagents.name: constant potential electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction initiates at ~0.8 V (vs SCE), below styrene oxidation potential, indicating initiation by oxidation of 1a/MeOH rather than styrene

notes: A minor pathway is proposed in which an anodically generated methoxy radical performs HAT on 1a to furnish the N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical A (Scheme 5, 'minor' arrow); the major pathway goes through direct anodic oxidation of deprotonated 1a'. Initiation by alkene oxidation is ruled out based on CV (styrene oxidation potential is higher than the operating potential).
